mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2017 payroll level at 131,334 thousand suggests ongoing labor market expansion
regime_hint: post-recession expansion with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent, the monetary policy stance is accommodative, reducing the cost of capital and supporting equity valuations. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as long as the rate stays in the low regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of March 2017 indicates deep accommodation
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC of the trigger signal is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening consumer demand and construction activity. The joint expansion of these two demand channels produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 395,472 million and HOUST at 571 thousand units in Feb 2017 indicate dual demand strength
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS growth × HOUST growth) produces Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of joint RSAFS and HOUST growth do not produce significantly higher returns than periods of single-channel growth (t-test p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints. This contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates and equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.04 in Feb 2017 reflects post-recession supply normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months when INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC of the INDPRO decline signal is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% in March 2017 reflects post-crisis labor market recovery
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC of the unemployment rise signal is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% annually, combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 2.0%, creates a negative real interest rate regime that erodes fixed-income returns and redirects capital to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 226.27 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% in Feb-Mar 2017 indicate negative real rates
regime_hint: low nominal rates with rising inflation creating negative real rate environment
failure_condition: The joint condition (inflation > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0%) produces Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns are not significantly higher in this regime versus baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency above 2.0% annualized signals sustained economic expansion. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings growth accelerates in line with GDP expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,271.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2016 reflects post-recession recovery
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth signal falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0% do not produce significantly higher forward 60-day returns than quarters with lower growth (t-test p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) both accelerating month-over-month signals synchronized supply and labor expansion, indicating robust productive capacity growth. This interaction mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as unit labor costs decline and profit margins expand.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.04 and PAYEMS at 131,334 thousand in Feb-Mar 2017 show synchronized expansion
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor expansion with productivity gains
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth × PAYEMS growth) produces Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of joint INDPRO and PAYEMS acceleration do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than periods of single-series acceleration (t-test p > 0.08).
